message:
refactor(render): introduce PreparedContent enum and optimize presenter hot paths

Restructure cell content emission in the presenter for better clarity,
branch prediction, and reduced indirection:

- Add PreparedContent enum (Empty | Char | Grapheme) that classifies cell
  content once, up front, before emission. This avoids redundant method
  calls on CellContent during the emit phase and makes the three code
  paths explicit and self-documenting.

- Move width computation into PreparedContent::from_cell() so the raw
  width is determined alongside the content classification in a single
  pass rather than being computed separately.

- Rewrite emit_content() to match on PreparedContent instead of probing
  the cell with grapheme_id() / as_char() sequentially. Each arm is now
  independent and easier to reason about.

- Add a dedicated ASCII fast-path inside the Char arm: ASCII characters
  are emitted as a single byte write instead of going through the
  4-byte encode_utf8 buffer, and ASCII control characters are sanitized
  to a space byte directly without the generic is_control() check.

- Reverse the comparison order in cost_model::digit_count() so the
  common case (small terminal coordinates) is tested first, reducing
  average branch mispredictions on the planner's hottest path.

- Simplify hyperlink bookkeeping: remove the conditional guard around
  emit_link_changes() that skipped the call when no link registry
  existed and no link was active. The method itself already handles
  the no-op case, and the conditional added complexity without
  measurable benefit.

- Add two unit tests: emit_content_ascii_char_emits_single_byte and
  emit_content_ascii_control_sanitizes_to_space, covering the new ASCII
  fast-path in the Char arm.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
